metadata.seed: I met Ivy a couple of years ago at the park. It only took one glimpse to feel my heart sparked. We kicked off things slowly, its been two years now. She confessed her love to me and I did the same. I was planning to propose to her when i found out she is pregnant with another mans child. I am broken, my future is no more and I feel my life is meaningless

patient.query: I met Ivy a couple of years ago at the park. It only took one glimpse to feel my heart sparked. We kicked off things slowly, its been two years now. She confessed her love to me and I did the same. I was planning to propose to her when i found out she is pregnant with another mans child. I am broken, my future is no more and I feel my life is meaningless

triggers: finding out she is pregnant with another man's child
automatic_thoughts: my future is no more
I feel my life is meaningless

llm_response.response: I'm really sorry to hear about the pain you're going through, Ivy. It sounds like this news has left you feeling broken and that your future seems meaningless right now. Let's take some time to explore these feelings together and see if we can find a way forward.

patient.query: Thanks. I guess I just feel... lost. It’s like everything I thought I knew about us just crumbled. I thought we were on the same page, and now I’m not sure what I’m supposed to do next. It’s hard to see a way forward when it feels like my whole world just flipped upside down.

triggers: not sure what to do next
automatic_thoughts: thought we were on the same page
hard to see a way forward

patient.query: Yeah, it really is overwhelming. I keep replaying everything in my mind—like, did I miss the signs? Was there something I could have done differently? I just feel this weight of confusion and regret, and it's hard to breathe sometimes, you know? I want to move on, but part of me just feels stuck in all these memories.

was there something I could have done differently?

patient.query: I don’t know… It’s like there were little things that I brushed off at the time. Maybe they were bigger than I realized. Like when they started pulling away or didn’t want to talk as much. I kept telling myself it was just a phase, but I wonder if I could have confronted it sooner, you know? I just keep thinking if I had said something, maybe things would have turned out differently. But then again, maybe it was just inevitable. It’s so frustrating to

patient.query: Yeah, there was this one time when we were supposed to go out, and they just... canceled last minute. They said they had a lot on their mind and needed space. At first, I was worried about them, but then it felt like I was being pushed away, you know? I felt so helpless and just sat there, questioning if I was being too needy or if I was losing them. It hurt a lot, and instead of talking about it, I just bottled it up.

automatic_thoughts: I am being too needy
I am losing them
I should not express my feelings

It's just frustrating to think that maybe I could have done something to change that.
